title: Bothriospora corymbosa
taxonomy: Bothriospora corymbosa is classified as a species of lichenized fungus in the family Ramalinaceae. It belongs to the genus Bothriospora, which is a part of the order Lecanorales. This classification places it within the class Lecanoromycetes and the phylum Ascomycota. The family Ramalinaceae is a group of fungi characterized by their crustose or squamulose thallus and lecanorine apothecia. Bothriospora corymbosa's classification reflects its unique characteristics and relationships within the fungal kingdom.
uses: Bothriospora corymbosa is reported to have potential medicinal uses, primarily due to its unique chemical composition.
